vectors.name: Procedure (Declaration and Call)
vectors.description: A procedure is a subprogram that performs a specific task but does not return a value. It is invoked as a standalone statement.

quirks_and_tests.description: Validates basic procedure declaration, invocation, and execution flow.

vectors.name: Function (Declaration and Return)
vectors.description: A function is a subprogram that computes and returns a single value. In Turbo Pascal, there is no 'return' keyword. The return value is specified by assigning a value to the function's own identifier before it exits.

quirks_and_tests.description: Validates function execution and the assignment to the function identifier for returning a value.

vectors.name: Parameters (Pass by Value)
vectors.description: In Turbo Pascal, parameters are passed by value by default. The procedure or function receives a copy of the argument. Any modifications made to the parameter within the routine are strictly local and do not affect the original variable passed by the caller.

quirks_and_tests.description: Validation of memory isolation for value parameters.

vectors.name: VAR Parameters (Pass by Reference)
vectors.description: To pass a parameter by reference, the VAR keyword must precede the parameter declaration. The routine accesses the actual memory address of the argument. Any changes made to the parameter inside the routine will directly modify the original variable.

quirks_and_tests.description: Validation of memory mutation for reference parameters.

vectors.name: Scope (Global Visibility)
vectors.description: Variables declared in the main VAR block of a program are global. They are visible and can be directly accessed or modified by any procedure or function, provided they are declared before the routine in the source code.

quirks_and_tests.description: Validation of outer scope traversal and mutation.

vectors.name: Scope (Local and Variable Shadowing)
vectors.description: Variables declared within a procedure or function are local to that routine. If a local variable has the exact same name as a global variable, the local declaration 'shadows' (hides) the global one. Any operations on that identifier within the routine will only affect the local instance.

code: X := 99; { Modifies local X, not global X }

quirks_and_tests.description: Validation of scope priority (Local over Global) during identifier resolution.
